data.title: As a DSO agent, post-all.sh hook auto-accumulates measured tokens on the active ticket
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend `post-all.sh` hook to extract per-turn token counts from the Claude Code hook payload and write them to an active ticket via `ticket-edit.sh --tokens_measured=`.

**Why**: Without automatic extraction, every agent and skill would need to manually emit token counts, which they will not do reliably. The hook runs on every tool call completion and can accumulate per-session token deltas.

**Scope**:
- IN: Parse `post-all.sh` hook payload for token count fields (input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_creation_tokens).
- IN: Active-ticket detection — read a session-local file (e.g. `/tmp/dso-active-ticket-<pid>`) or env var that skills set when beginning work on a specific ticket.
- IN: Accumulate tokens for the session and write periodically (every N tool calls or at session end) to the active ticket.
- OUT: Retroactive token attribution for past sessions.
- OUT: Planning-vs-execution separation — planning skills emit tokens_estimated in their own story.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a sprint session with an active ticket accumulates `tokens_measured` on that ticket visible via `ticket show`.
  ← Satisfies: "Measure execution token use on the worked ticket"
- When this story is complete, closing the active ticket stops accumulation for that ticket and resumes if a new active ticket is declared.
  ← Satisfies: "Measure execution token use on the worked ticket"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Hook failure must not block tool use — fail-open if token extraction errors.
- [Performance] Write frequency balance — too frequent writes bloat the event log.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
